public_functions.behavior_description: Enable the GTM and its FXCLK domain, then initialize the unified IfxGtm_Pwm driver to generate center-aligned PWM at 20 kHz on a synchronized multi-channel group. Assign the required TOM outputs to the configured I/O pins using the generic GTM pin-map API with proper output mode and pad driver. Configure exactly the channels needed for 3-phase operation on the selected TOM instance, ensuring synchronous shadow update is enabled. Initialize the PWM configuration struct with defaults, set the operating frequency and center-aligned mode, and create per-channel configurations mapped to the selected TOM submodule channels. Apply the initial duty commands for the three phases as 25%, 50%, and 75% using the unified duty update API. Start the synchronized PWM outputs so all channels run with a common time base. For unused complementary outputs (if any are present on the board but not required now), set them to a safe GPIO state (push-pull, driven low) to avoid unintended switching.

Maintain and update three phase duty commands over time. On each invocation, check whether the 500 ms interval has elapsed (using a software counter or timestamp accumulated across calls). When 500 ms have passed, increment each phase duty by +10 percentage points. If a duty value reaches or exceeds 100%, wrap it back within the 0–100% range; specifically, clamp the request so that an exact 100% command never produces a full-period match by reducing the request to just below one full period (equivalent to a period-minus-1-tick condition in the underlying timer domain). Store the updated duty values and apply them atomically to all configured channels using the immediate multi-channel duty update API to keep the waveforms synchronized.
